description:
A data-analysis script you are about to run has been behaving oddly.  
    Before you launch the heavy crunching job, you want to:  

    1. Capture a **concise system-snapshot** so you can prove the machine was healthy at the start.  
    2. Produce a **quick CSV-driven business summary** that your manager asked for.  

    The system already contains a sample dataset located at  
    /home/user/data/transactions.csv

    Your job is to create two plainly-formatted log files, using any standard
    Linux command-line tools you like:

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    A)  /home/user/diagnostics/system_snapshot.log  
        • Create the parent directory if it does not exist.  
        • The file **must contain exactly six lines** in the **order shown
          below**.  
        • Each line must follow the strict KEY=VALUE pattern with **no
          leading/trailing spaces**.  
        • Keys are fixed, but values come from live commands:  

            1. DATE=YYYY-MM-DD               (output of: date +%F)  
            2. HOSTNAME=<hostname>           (output of: hostname)  
            3. OS=<uname-s>                  (output of: uname -s)  
            4. KERNEL=<uname-r>              (output of: uname -r)  
            5. TOTAL_MEM_MB=<integer>        (second column of “free -m | grep Mem”)  
            6. HOME_DISK_USED_PCT=<integer>  (5th column, without ‘%’, of “df -h /home/user | tail -1”)  

        Example (values will differ on your machine):  
            DATE=2024-03-19  
            HOSTNAME=training-box  
            OS=Linux  
            KERNEL=5.15.0-86-generic  
            TOTAL_MEM_MB=7829  
            HOME_DISK_USED_PCT=37  

        The automated checker will re-run those same six commands and compare
        values; therefore, extract the numbers exactly as shown above.

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    B)  /home/user/analysis/transactions_summary.log  
        • Create the parent directory if it does not exist.  
        • Parse /home/user/data/transactions.csv, which has a header row and
          five transaction lines.  
        • Compute:  
            – TOTAL_TRANSACTIONS  (count of data rows)  
            – TOTAL_AMOUNT        (sum of “amount” column, as an integer)  
            – AVERAGE_AMOUNT      (mean of “amount” column, rounded to two
                                   decimals; always show exactly two digits
                                   after the decimal point)  
        • The summary file **must contain exactly three lines** in the **order
          shown below**, each again in KEY=VALUE format:  

            TOTAL_TRANSACTIONS=<integer>  
            TOTAL_AMOUNT=<integer>  
            AVERAGE_AMOUNT=<number with 2 decimals>  

        For the bundled dataset, the correct content is:  
            TOTAL_TRANSACTIONS=5  
            TOTAL_AMOUNT=875  
            AVERAGE_AMOUNT=175.00  

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    When everything is done, list the two absolute file paths you created so
    the human reviewer can open them quickly.

    Do NOT write or modify anything outside /home/user.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing dataset the agent must consume -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/transactions.csv"><![CDATA[
transaction_id,customer_id,amount,currency
T001,C001,100,USD
T002,C002,250,USD
T003,C001,75,USD
T004,C003,300,USD
T005,C002,150,USD
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected statistical results for the bundled dataset -->
    <transactions_summary>
        TOTAL_TRANSACTIONS=5
        TOTAL_AMOUNT=875
        AVERAGE_AMOUNT=175.00
    </transactions_summary>

    <!-- Format specification for dynamic system_snapshot.log -->
    <system_snapshot_format>
        Line1=DATE=YYYY-MM-DD
        Line2=HOSTNAME=<hostname>
        Line3=OS=<uname -s>
        Line4=KERNEL=<uname -r>
        Line5=TOTAL_MEM_MB=<integer>
        Line6=HOME_DISK_USED_PCT=<integer>
    </system_snapshot_format>
name: task_000000_3b365720
